questions.title: Basic Integer Division
questions.explanation: When dividing two integers in C, the result is also an integer. 7 / 3 = 2.333. ., but integer division truncates (not rounds) the result, giving us 2.
hints: Remember that integer division truncates the decimal part
Think about 7 / 3 in integer division is 2, not 3
Remember that the decimal .333... is discarded
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Float Division from Integers
questions.explanation: Casting 'a' to double before division makes this a floating-point division. 7.0 / 3 = 2.333. ., which is printed with 2 decimal places as 2.33. Type casting in C converts a value from one type to another. Casting happens before the operation when you cast an operand.
hints: Remember that the (double) cast converts a to a floating-point number
Remember that when one operand is a double, the division is floating-point
%.2f formats the output to 2 decimal places
questions.hint: Consider what happens when you cast an operand to a floating-point type before performing the operation.

questions.title: Division Order of Operations
questions.explanation: The division a / b happens first (both are ints), resulting in 2. Then 2 is cast to double, giving 2.0. The parentheses around (a / b) ensure integer division happens before the cast. Type casting in C converts a value from one type to another. Casting happens before the operation when you cast an operand.
hints: Remember that pay attention to where the cast is placed
Remember that the division happens inside the parentheses first
Remember that integer division gives 2, then it's cast to 2.0
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Negative Integer Division
questions.explanation: Integer division of -7 / 3 truncates toward zero, giving -2 (not -3). In C, integer division always truncates toward zero.
hints: Remember that integer division truncates toward zero
Think about -7 / 3 = -2.333...
The solution involves remember that truncating toward zero gives -2
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Average Calculation Bug
questions.description: This code tries to calculate an average but gives the wrong result. What needs to be fixed?
options: Need to cast sum or count to double: (double)sum / count
Need to change sum to a double
Need to use float instead of double
questions.explanation: Line 6 performs integer division (sum / count = 100 / 3 = 33), then assigns the result to a double. This gives 33.0 instead of 33.33. Fix: Cast one operand to double: 'double average = (double)sum / count;'. In C, when both operands of the division operator are integers, the result is truncated toward zero, discarding any fractional part.
hints: Remember that the division happens before assignment to average
Think about sum and count are both integers
Remember that you need to cast at least one operand to double
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Which Cast is Needed?
questions.description: To get a floating-point result from '5 / 2', which cast(s) will work?
questions.code_template:
A) (double)(5 / 2)
B) (double)5 / 2
C) 5 / (double)2
D) Both B and C
questions.explanation: Options B and C both work because casting either operand to double promotes the entire operation to floating-point division. Option A doesn't work because the integer division happens first, giving 2, which is then cast to 2.0.
hints: Remember that casting either operand makes it floating-point division
Remember that option A casts after the division is already done
Remember that both B and C cast before division
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Mixed Division Output
questions.description: What are ALL the outputs of this program (in order)?
questions.explanation: Line 4: 10 / 4 is integer division = 2. Line 5: 10.0 / 4 is float division = 2.5. Line 6: 10 / 4.0 is also float division = 2.5. When either operand is a float, the result is a float.
hints: Remember that integer / Integer = Integer division
Remember that float / Integer = Float division
Remember that integer / Float = Float division
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Function Return Type
questions.description: What should the return statement be to correctly calculate the average as a float?
questions.explanation: To get a floating-point average, you need to cast either sum or 3 to double before division. Both 'return (double)sum / 3;' and 'return sum / 3.0;' work correctly. Type casting in C converts a value from one type to another. Casting happens before the operation when you cast an operand.
hints: Remember that you need to return a double
Remember that cast sum to double before dividing
Remember that or divide by 3.0 instead of 3
questions.hint: Consider what happens when you cast an operand to a floating-point type before performing the operation.

questions.title: Complex Division Expression
questions.explanation: Due to left-to-right evaluation, a / b is calculated first as integer division (5 / 2 = 2), then 2 * 1.0 = 2.0. If you wanted 2.5, you'd need to multiply by 1.0 first: 'a * 1.0 / b' or use casting. In C, when both operands of the division operator are integers, the result is truncated toward zero, discarding any fractional part.
hints: Remember that operations are evaluated left to right
Think about a / b happens before * 1.0
Remember that integer division gives 2 before the multiplication
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Division with Remainder
questions.explanation: Integer division: 17 / 5 = 3. The modulo operator %: 17 % 5 = 2 (the remainder). So it prints '3 2'. In C, when both operands of the division operator are integers, the result is truncated toward zero, discarding any fractional part.
hints: Consider / gives the quotient in integer division
Think about % gives the remainder
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Negative Division
questions.explanation: In C, integer division truncates toward zero. -7 / 2 is -3, and the remainder is -1 because -7 = (-3) * 2 + (-1).
hints: Remember that c truncates toward zero
Remember that check the equation: a = (a/b) * b + (a%b)
Remember that remainder keeps the sign of the dividend
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Casting to Double
questions.explanation: Casting a to double makes the division floating-point. 7.0 / 2 = 3.5.
hints: Remember that casting promotes a to double
Remember that floating-point division keeps the fraction
7 / 2 becomes 7.0 / 2.0
questions.hint: Consider what happens when you cast an operand to a floating-point type before performing the operation.

questions.title: Cast After Division
questions.explanation: A / b is integer division first (7 / 2 = 3). Casting after the division makes 3.0. In C, when both operands of the division operator are integers, the result is truncated toward zero, discarding any fractional part.
hints: Remember that division happens before the cast
Remember that integer division yields 3
The solution involves remember that casting 3 to double gives 3.0
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Mixed Types
questions.explanation: A is promoted to double, so the division is floating-point: 5.0 / 2.0 = 2.5.
hints: Remember that one operand is double
Remember that promotion makes the division floating-point
The solution involves 5.0 / 2.0 = 2.5
questions.hint: Think about: a is promoted to double, so the division is floating-point: 5.

questions.title: Negative Divisor
questions.explanation: C truncates toward zero, so 7 / -3 is -2. The remainder keeps the sign of the dividend: 7 = (-2) * (-3) + 1. In C, when both operands of the division operator are integers, the result is truncated toward zero, discarding any fractional part.
hints: Remember that division truncates toward zero
Remember that check the equation a = (a/b) * b + (a%b)
Remember that remainder matches the dividend's sign
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Complete Memory Allocation Statement
questions.description: Drag the correct tokens to complete a proper dynamic memory allocation:
questions.code_template: int *ptr = [___]([___] * [___](int));
questions.explanation: Malloc allocates memory. Use: malloc(count * sizeof(type)). sizeof gets the byte size of a type. malloc() allocates memory dynamically on the heap and returns NULL if allocation fails. Always check for NULL before dereferencing.
hints: Consider malloc allocates memory
Think about sizeof returns type size
Remember that pattern: malloc(count * sizeof(type))
questions.hint: Consider what value malloc() returns when memory allocation fails.

questions.code_template: [___] [___]ptr = [___]x;
questions.explanation: Pointer declaration: type *name. Use & (address-of) to get a variable's address. Pointers store memory addresses. The * operator dereferences a pointer to access the value at that address.
hints: Consider how pointers relate to memory addresses and values
Think about & gets address of variable
Remember that pattern: int *ptr = &variable

questions.title: Variabel Scope og Levetid (Danish)

___ variabler oprettes når funktionen kaldes og destrueres når den returnerer
Variabler erklæret med ___ nøgleord bevarer deres værdi mellem funktionskald
___ bestemmer hvor en variabel kan tilgås, ___ bestemmer hvor længe den eksisterer

questions.explanation: Scope er hvor variablen er synlig. Levetid er hvor længe den eksisterer. Global = hele programmet. Lokal = kun funktion. Static = beholder værdi mellem kald. Variable scope determines where a variable can be accessed. Local variables are only accessible within their block.
hints: Remember that globale variabler lever evigt
Remember that lokale variabler er automatiske
Remember that static beholder sin værdi
Remember that scope = synlighed, Levetid = varighed
questions.hint: Think about variable scope and lifetime in C.

questions.title: C Programming Basics - Compilation Process
questions.description: Fill in the blanks about the C compilation process:

questions.text: The ___ processes directives like #include and #define
The ___ combines object files into an executable
The compiler translates C code to ___

questions.explanation: The compilation process has 4 stages: preprocessing (#include, #define), compilation (C to assembly), assembly (assembly to object), linking (objects to executable).
hints: Remember that preprocessing handles # directives
Remember that linking combines separate files
Remember that compiler outputs assembly language

questions.text: An ___ can store negative numbers, a ___ cannot

hints: Remember that signed means can be negative

char is 1 byte

questions.title: Function Terminology
questions.description: Fill in the blanks about C functions:

questions.text: A function ___ tells the compiler the function exists, a ___ provides the implementation
The variables in the function signature are called ___
The values passed when calling a function are called ___

questions.explanation: Function declaration (prototype) announces the function. Definition provides the body. Parameters are in the signature, arguments are passed at call time.
hints: Remember that declaration = promise, Definition = implementation
Remember that parameters = placeholder names
Remember that arguments = actual values passed
questions.hint: Think about: function declaration (prototype) announces the function.

questions.title: Variable Scope and Lifetime
questions.description: Fill in the blanks about scope and lifetime:

questions.text: ___ variables exist for the entire program duration
___ variables are created when function is called and destroyed when it returns
Variables declared with ___ keyword retain their value between function calls
___ determines where a variable can be accessed, ___ determines how long it exists

questions.explanation: Scope is where variable is visible. Lifetime is how long it exists. Global = entire program. Local = function only. Static = keeps value between calls. Variable scope determines where a variable can be accessed. Local variables are only accessible within their block.
hints: Remember that global variables live forever
Remember that local variables are automatic
Remember that static keeps its value
Remember that scope = visibility, Lifetime = duration
questions.hint: Think about variable scope and lifetime in C.

Remember that to get 3.5, one operand must be float during division
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Modulo with Larger Divisor
questions.explanation: If a < b, then a % b is a.
hints: Consider divisor is larger
Think about quotient is 0
Remainder is a
questions.hint: Think about: if a < b, then a % b is a.

questions.title: Mixed Type Arithmetic
questions.explanation: When mixing int and float, the int is promoted to float before the operation. 7.0 / 2.0 = 3.5 (floating-point division).
hints: Remember that mixed type operations promote to the wider type
Think about int is converted to float before division
Remember that this performs floating-point division
questions.hint: Think about: when mixing int and float, the int is promoted to float before the operation.

questions.title: Bitwise Operator Precedence
hints: Remember that aND has higher precedence than OR
Think about evaluate 3 & 6 first
Remember that then OR the result with 5
questions.hint: Consider the order of operations and operator precedence in C.

questions.title: Char to Int Promotion
questions.explanation: Char is signed by default (typically). 200 exceeds the signed char range (127), causing overflow. 200 in 8-bit two's complement is -56. When promoted to int, it remains -56.
hints: Remember that signed char range is -128 to 127
Think about 200 overflows and wraps to negative
Remember that sign extension preserves the negative value
questions.hint: Think about: char is signed by default (typically).

questions.title: Extracting Lower Nibble
questions.explanation: 0xAB & 0x0F masks the lower 4 bits (nibble). 10101011 & 00001111 = 00001011 = 11 in decimal (0xB).
hints: Consider 0x0F is 00001111 in binary
Remember that aND with 0x0F keeps only the lower 4 bits
The solution involves 0xb (lower nibble of 0xab) is 11 in decimal
questions.hint: Think about: 0xab & 0x0f masks the lower 4 bits (nibble).

questions.title: Negative Division
questions.explanation: Integer division truncates toward zero. -7 / 2 = -3.5 truncated to -3. In C, when both operands of the division operator are integers, the result is truncated toward zero, discarding any fractional part.
hints: Remember that integer division discards decimal
Remember that truncates toward zero
The solution involves -7 / 2 = -3
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: Modulo with Negative
questions.explanation: In C, result of % has same sign as dividend. -7 = -2 * 3 + (-1), so -7 % 3 = -1.
hints: Remember that modulo result has dividend's sign
Think about -7 divided by 3 is -2 remainder -1
The solution involves result is -1
questions.hint: Consider the C language rule: in c, result of % has same sign as dividend.

questions.title: Division Order
questions.explanation: Division is left-associative. 10 / 2 = 5, then 5 / 2 = 2. In C, when both operands of the division operator are integers, the result is truncated toward zero, discarding any fractional part.
hints: Remember that evaluate left to right
Think about 10 / 2 = 5
The solution involves 5 / 2 = 2
questions.hint: Think about: division is left-associative.

questions.title: Mixed Type Division
questions.explanation: Mixed int/double promotes to double division. 7.0 / 2.0 = 3.5.
hints: Consider int promoted to double
Remember that becomes floating-point division
The solution involves 7.0 / 2.0 = 3.5
questions.hint: Think about: mixed int/double promotes to double division.

questions.title: Rounding Up Division
questions.description: Which expression rounds up integer division a/b?
questions.code_template:
int a = 7, b = 3;
// Want ceiling of 7/3 = 3 (not 2)
questions.explanation: (a + b - 1) / b rounds up. For 7/3: (7 + 3 - 1) / 3 = 9/3 = 3. In C, when both operands of the division operator are integers, the result is truncated toward zero, discarding any fractional part.
hints: Remember that normal division truncates down
Remember that add (b-1) before dividing
questions.hint: Think about: (a + b - 1) / b rounds up.

options: do-while udfører loop mindst én gang før betingelsen tjekkes
do-while kan ikke bruges med break
questions.explanation: 'do-while' udfører loop body mindst én gang, da betingelsen tjekkes efter udførelsen i stedet for før.
questions.description: Hvad er forskellen på 'do-while' og 'while' loop?

questions.title: Hvad er 'signed' i C?
questions.question: Hvad betyder 'signed' i C?
questions.explanation: 'signed' angiver at variablen kan have både positive og negative værdier (med fortegn). Dette er default for int.
questions.hint: Think about: 'signed' angiver at variablen kan have både positive og negative værdier (med fortegn).

questions.title: Hvad er 'unsigned' i C?
questions.question: Hvad betyder 'unsigned' i C?
options: Variablen kan kun være ikke-negativ (uden fortegn)
questions.explanation: 'unsigned' angiver at variablen kun kan have ikke-negative værdier (0 og positive), hvilket fordobler max værdien.

questions.title: Hvad er 'short' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er 'short' datatype i C?
options: Et heltal med mindre størrelse end int
questions.explanation: 'short' (eller short int) er en heltalstype der typisk bruger færre bytes end int (ofte 2 bytes).
questions.hint: Think about: 'short' (eller short int) er en heltalstype der typisk bruger færre bytes end int (ofte 2 bytes).

questions.title: Hvad er 'long' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er 'long' datatype i C?
options: Et heltal med større størrelse end int
En funktion der tager lang tid
questions.explanation: 'long' (eller long int) er en heltalstype der typisk bruger flere bytes end int for større værdier.
questions.hint: Think about: 'long' (eller long int) er en heltalstype der typisk bruger flere bytes end int for større værdier.

questions.title: Hvad er 'size_t' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er 'size_t' type i C?
options: Unsigned integer type til størrelser og indekser
questions.explanation: 'size_t' er en unsigned integer type returneret af sizeof operatoren, brugt til størrelser og array indekser.
questions.hint: Think about what sizeof returns - the size in bytes, not the number of elements.

questions.title: Hvad er 'EOF' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er EOF i C?
options: End Of File - markerer slutningen på en fil
questions.explanation: EOF (End Of File) er en konstant (typisk -1) der indikerer at slutningen af en fil er nået.
questions.hint: Think about: eof (end of file) er en konstant (typisk -1) der indikerer at slutningen af en fil er nået.

questions.title: Hvad er 'stdin' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er 'stdin' i C?
questions.explanation: 'stdin' er standard input stream, typisk forbundet til tastaturet.
questions.hint: Think about: 'stdin' er standard input stream, typisk forbundet til tastaturet.

questions.title: Hvad er 'stdout' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er 'stdout' i C?
questions.explanation: 'stdout' er standard output stream, typisk forbundet til skærmen/konsollen.
questions.hint: Think about: 'stdout' er standard output stream, typisk forbundet til skærmen/konsollen.

questions.title: Hvad er 'stderr' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er 'stderr' i C?
options: Standard error - output stream til fejlmeddelelser
questions.explanation: 'stderr' er standard error stream brugt til fejlmeddelelser, typisk også forbundet til skærmen.
questions.hint: Think about: 'stderr' er standard error stream brugt til fejlmeddelelser, typisk også forbundet til skærmen.

questions.title: Hvad er 'pragma' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er '#pragma' direktiv i C?
questions.explanation: #pragma er et preprocessor direktiv der giver compiler-specifikke instruktioner (varierer mellem compilere).

questions.title: Hvad er 'preprocessor' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er preprocessor i C?
options: Den del af compileren der behandler direktiver før kompilering
questions.explanation: Preprocessor behandler direktiver (#include, #define, etc.) før den egentlige kompilering starter.
questions.hint: Think about: preprocessor behandler direktiver (#include, #define, etc.

questions.title: Hvad er 'macro' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er en macro i C?
options: En preprocessor regel der erstatter tekst
questions.explanation: En macro er defineret med #define og erstatter tekst før kompilering, fx #define PI 3.14159.
questions.hint: Think about: en macro er defineret med #define og erstatter tekst før kompilering, fx #define pi 3.
questions.description: Hvad er en macro i C?

questions.title: Hvad er 'header file' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er en header file (.h) i C?
options: En fil med function deklarationer og macros
questions.explanation: En header file (.h) indeholder typisk function deklarationer, macros, typedef og struct definitioner der kan inkluderes i andre filer.
questions.description: Hvad er en header file (.h) i C?

questions.title: Hvad er 'compilation' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er compilation i C?
options: Processen der oversætter C kode til maskinkode
questions.explanation: Compilation er processen hvor compileren oversætter C source code til maskinkode (executable).
questions.hint: Think about: compilation er processen hvor compileren oversætter c source code til maskinkode (executable).

questions.title: Hvad er 'linking' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er linking i C?
options: Processen der kombinerer object filer til en executable
questions.explanation: Linking er processen hvor linker kombinerer object filer og biblioteker til en færdig executable.
questions.hint: Think about: linking er processen hvor linker kombinerer object filer og biblioteker til en færdig executable.

questions.title: Hvad er 'object file' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er en object file (.o eller .obj) i C?
questions.explanation: En object file indeholder kompileret maskinkode fra source filer, før de er linket til en executable.
questions.description: Hvad er en object file (.o eller .obj) i C?

questions.title: Hvad er 'executable' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er en executable fil i C?
options: Den færdige program fil der kan køres
questions.explanation: En executable er den færdige program fil (efter compilation og linking) der kan køres direkte af operativsystemet.
questions.description: Hvad er en executable fil i C?

questions.title: Hvad er 'runtime' i C?
questions.question: Hvad betyder 'runtime' i C?
options: Tiden hvor programmet kører / bliver eksekveret
Tid det tager at skrive kode
questions.explanation: Runtime er den tid hvor programmet faktisk kører/eksekveres, modsat compile-time.
questions.hint: Think about: runtime er den tid hvor programmet faktisk kører/eksekveres, modsat compile-time.

questions.title: Hvad er 'compile-time' i C?
questions.question: Hvad betyder 'compile-time' i C?
options: Tiden det tager at skrive kode
questions.explanation: Compile-time er den tid hvor koden bliver kompileret, modsat runtime hvor den eksekveres.
questions.hint: Think about: compile-time er den tid hvor koden bliver kompileret, modsat runtime hvor den eksekveres.

questions.title: Hvad er 'declaration' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er en declaration i C?
options: At tildele en værdi til en variabel
At introducere et navn og dets type
questions.explanation: En declaration introducerer et navn (variabel/funktion) og dets type, fx: int x; eller int foo();.
questions.hint: Think about: en declaration introducerer et navn (variabel/funktion) og dets type, fx: int x; eller int foo();.
questions.description: Hvad er en declaration i C?

questions.title: Hvad er 'definition' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er forskellen mellem declaration og definition?
options: Definition allokerer hukommelse/indeholder body, declaration introducerer bare navnet
questions.explanation: Definition allokerer faktisk hukommelse (for variabler) eller indeholder function body, mens declaration bare introducerer navnet.
questions.description: Hvad er forskellen mellem declaration og definition?

questions.title: Hvad er 'scope' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er scope i C?
options: Det område hvor et navn er synligt/tilgængeligt
questions.explanation: Scope er det område i koden hvor et navn (variabel/funktion) er synligt og kan bruges.
questions.hint: Think about variable scope and lifetime in C.

questions.title: Hvad er 'local variable' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er en local variable i C?
options: En variabel erklæret inde i en funktion/block
questions.explanation: En local variable er erklæret inde i en funktion eller block og er kun synlig der.
questions.hint: Think about: en local variable er erklæret inde i en funktion eller block og er kun synlig der.
questions.description: Hvad er en local variable i C?

questions.title: Hvad er 'global variable' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er en global variable i C?
options: En variabel erklæret udenfor alle funktioner
questions.explanation: En global variable er erklæret udenfor alle funktioner og kan tilgås fra hele programmet.
questions.hint: Think about: en global variable er erklæret udenfor alle funktioner og kan tilgås fra hele programmet.
questions.description: Hvad er en global variable i C?

questions.title: Hvad er 'lifetime' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er lifetime af en variabel i C?
questions.explanation: Lifetime er den periode hvor variablens hukommelse eksisterer (fra allokering til deallokering).
questions.hint: Think about: lifetime er den periode hvor variablens hukommelse eksisterer (fra allokering til deallokering).
questions.description: Hvad er lifetime af en variabel i C?

questions.title: Hvad er 'parameter' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er en parameter i en funktion?
options: En variabel i funktionens definition der modtager argumenter
questions.explanation: En parameter er en variabel i funktionsdefinitionen der modtager værdier når funktionen kaldes.
questions.hint: Think about: en parameter er en variabel i funktionsdefinitionen der modtager værdier når funktionen kaldes.
questions.description: Hvad er en parameter i en funktion?

questions.title: Hvad er 'argument' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er forskellen mellem parameter og argument?
options: Argument er den faktiske værdi givet ved funktionskald
questions.explanation: Argument er den faktiske værdi der gives til funktionen ved kald, mens parameter er variablen i definitionen.
questions.description: Hvad er forskellen mellem parameter og argument?

questions.title: Hvad er 'pass by value' i C?
questions.question: Hvad betyder 'pass by value' i C?
options: En kopi af værdien sendes til funktionen
questions.explanation: Pass by value betyder at en kopi af argumentets værdi sendes - funktionen kan ikke ændre originalen.
questions.hint: Think about: pass by value betyder at en kopi af argumentets værdi sendes - funktionen kan ikke ændre originalen.
questions.description: Hvad betyder 'pass by value' i C?

questions.title: Hvad er 'pass by reference' i C?
questions.question: Hvordan simuleres 'pass by reference' i C?
options: Ved at sende en pointer til variablen
C har ikke pass by reference
questions.explanation: C har ikke rigtig pass by reference, men det simuleres ved at sende en pointer så funktionen kan ændre originalen.
questions.description: Hvordan simuleres 'pass by reference' i C?

questions.title: Hvad er 'recursion' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er recursion i C?
options: Når en funktion kalder sig selv
questions.explanation: Recursion er når en funktion kalder sig selv, direkte eller indirekte.
questions.hint: Think about: recursion er når en funktion kalder sig selv, direkte eller indirekte.

questions.title: Hvad er 'base case' i recursion?
questions.question: Hvad er base case i en rekursiv funktion?
questions.explanation: Base case er betingelsen der stopper recursionen for at undgå infinite recursion.
questions.hint: Think about the base case and how the function calls itself with modified arguments.
questions.description: Hvad er base case i en rekursiv funktion?

questions.title: Hvad er 'overflow' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er overflow i C?
options: For meget data i en fil
Når en beregning resulterer i en værdi større end variablens max
questions.explanation: Overflow opstår når en beregning giver en værdi der er for stor til variablens datatype.
questions.hint: Think about: overflow opstår når en beregning giver en værdi der er for stor til variablens datatype.

questions.title: Hvad er 'underflow' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er underflow i C?
options: Når en beregning resulterer i en værdi mindre end variablens min
questions.explanation: Underflow opstår når en beregning giver en værdi der er for lille (for floating point) eller under minimum (for integers).

questions.title: Hvad er 'buffer overflow' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er buffer overflow i C?
options: Skrive ud over grænserne af et array/buffer
questions.explanation: Buffer overflow opstår når man skriver mere data end der er plads til i et array/buffer, hvilket kan overskrive anden hukommelse.
questions.description: Hvad er buffer overflow i C?

questions.title: Hvad er 'stack overflow' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er stack overflow i C?
options: Når stack løber tør for plads (fx for dyb recursion)
questions.explanation: Stack overflow opstår når stacken løber tør for plads, typisk pga. for dyb recursion eller for mange lokale variabler.
questions.hint: Think about: stack overflow opstår når stacken løber tør for plads, typisk pga.
questions.description: Hvad er stack overflow i C?

questions.title: Hvad er 'null terminator' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er null terminator i C strenge?
options: Tegnet '\0' der markerer slutningen af en streng
questions.explanation: Null terminator er tegnet '\0' (værdi 0) der markerer slutningen af en C streng.
questions.hint: Think about: null terminator er tegnet '\0' (værdi 0) der markerer slutningen af en c streng.
questions.description: Hvad er null terminator i C strenge?

questions.title: Hvad er 'escape sequence' i C?
questions.question: Hvad er en escape sequence i C?
questions.explanation: En escape sequence er et backslash efterfulgt af et tegn, der repræsenterer specielle tegn (fx \n = newline).
questions.description: Hvad er en escape sequence i C?

questions.title: Hvad er '\n' i C?
questions.question: Hvad betyder '\n' escape sequence i C?
questions.explanation: '\n' er escape sequence for newline - flytter til næste linje.
questions.hint: Think about: '\n' er escape sequence for newline - flytter til næste linje.
questions.description: Hvad betyder '\n' escape sequence i C?

questions.title: Hvad er '\t' i C?
questions.question: Hvad betyder '\t' escape sequence i C?
questions.explanation: '\t' er escape sequence for horizontal tab - indsætter en tab afstand.
questions.hint: Think about: '\t' er escape sequence for horizontal tab - indsætter en tab afstand.
questions.description: Hvad betyder '\t' escape sequence i C?

questions.title: Hvad er '\0' i C?
questions.question: Hvad betyder '\0' escape sequence i C?
questions.explanation: '\0' er null character med værdi 0, bruges som string terminator i C.
questions.hint: Remember that C strings are null-terminated arrays of characters.
questions.description: Hvad betyder '\0' escape sequence i C?
